title: TeraWulf Signs 200+ MW, 10-Year AI Hosting Agreements with Fluidstack
text: TeraWulf will provide over 200 MW of critical IT load at its Lake Mariner data center in Western New York under a 10-year agreement with Fluidstack, an AI cloud platform provider. The initial 10-year contract value is approximately $3.7 billion in revenue, with two optional 5-year extensions potentially increasing total to approximately $8.7 billion. Expected annual net operating income is approximately $315 million with 85% margins. Project cost: $8-10 million per MW of critical IT load. Phase one (approximately 40 MW) is targeted for the first half of 2026, with the full 200+ MW deployed by year-end 2026. Google will backstop $1.8 billion of Fluidstack's lease obligations to support project debt and receive warrants for approximately 41 million shares, representing an 8% equity stake in TeraWulf. The Lake Mariner campus features dual 345 kV transmission lines, closed-loop water cooling designed for liquid-cooled AI workloads, and ultra-low-latency fiber connectivity. Fluidstack has been granted 30-day exclusivity for an additional 160 MW capacity (CB-5 facility).
